summary: Russian forces established control over Ivolzhanskoye in the Sumy region, while Ukraine struck a Russian naval arsenal near Saint Petersburg, destroying over 60,000 tons of ammunition. Russian 'Geranium' drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies in the Kharkov region, and a Russian MiG-31K carrier of Kinzhal missiles was tracked taking off from Savastleyka airfield. Russian forces intensified offensive activity across multiple eastern axes including Lyman, Kupyansk, and both South and North Slobozhansk directions.
key_points: Russian forces established control over Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region, with small-arms fighting continuing in Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, and Novaya Sech. (Source: @tass_agency/382236, @ukr_leaks_eng/31127)
Ukraine destroyed over 60,000 tons of ammunition at a Baltic Fleet arsenal near Saint Petersburg, according to President Zelensky. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)

Russia began transferring air defense systems from other regions to Moscow and the Kerch Bridge area due to Ukrainian strike threats, confirmed by internal Russian documents obtained by GUR. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)
Russian Geranium drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies for Ukrainian Armed Forces in the Kharkov region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135)
A Russian MiG-31K capable of carrying the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal ballistic missile took off from Savastleyka airfield, posing a significant threat. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026)
Russian forces intensified offensive activity across Lyman, Kupyansk, South Slobozhansk, and North Slobozhansk directions, per Ukrainian OS spokesman Trehubov. (Source: @UkraineNow/82138)
A Russian strike hit a beach in Zaporizhzhia, injuring four people including three children, according to the regional OVA. (Source: @UkraineNow/82150)
Russian strike killed a Norwegian People's Aid demining specialist and injured four others in Novopetrivka, Vysokopillya community, Kherson region. (Source: @UkraineNow/82149)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces established control over Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region while intensifying offensive activity across multiple eastern axes including Lyman, Kupyansk, and Slobozhansk directions. Ukraine struck a Russian Baltic Fleet arsenal near Saint Petersburg, destroying over 60,000 tons of ammunition, prompting Russia to redeploy air defense systems toward Moscow and Kerch Bridge. Russian Geranium drones struck military supply railcars in Kharkov region, and a MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier was launched from Savastleyka, while a beach strike in Zaporizhzhia injured four including three children.

points: Ukraine destroyed over 60,000 tons of ammunition at a Russian Baltic Fleet arsenal near Saint Petersburg, according to President Zelensky. This strike contributed to Russia transferring air defense systems from other regions to protect Moscow and the Kerch Bridge area. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)

The Ukrainian Armed Forces have begun using the domestically produced 'Vyravnyvatel' (Leveler) guided aerial bomb weighing 250 kg, with a range of 10–140 km when dropped from altitude. However, Russian aviation and air defense prevent Ukrainian aircraft from operating at the altitudes needed to achieve maximum range. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125)

Ukrainian forces deployed FPV-drone interception net systems on the Kostiantynivka direction, and a pump-action shotgun for drone destruction on the Kupyansk direction, funded through public donations. (Source: @eRadarrua/97961)
The State Border Service of Ukraine stated there is currently no ground invasion threat from Belarus, but confirmed Minsk is assisting Russia in launching drones. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31138)
A sudden blackout occurred across Russian-occupied territories of Kherson region, with the occupation governor stating all districts were partially or fully de-energized. (Source: @UkraineNow/82143)

Polish Prime Minister Tusk characterized Zelensky's absence from the Ukraine reconstruction conference in Gdansk as de-escalation, with the Ukrainian delegation led by Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31126)
Poland has begun denying temporary protection status to Ukrainians arriving from regions not affected by active military operations, according to Polish media reports. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31122)

The Russian Ministry of Defense reported that its forces liberated Ivolzhanskoye in the Sumy region, with small-arms fighting continuing in Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, and Novaya Sech. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127, @tass_agency/382236)
Russian Geranium drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies for Ukrainian Armed Forces in the Kharkov region, disrupting logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135)

A Russian MiG-31K, capable of launching the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic ballistic missile, took off from Savastleyka airfield, posing a significant threat to multiple regions. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026)

Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak reported to Putin that Russian petroleum refineries are operating at maximum capacity, requiring previously unused reserve mechanisms to stabilize domestic fuel supply. (Source: @UkraineNow/82141)
Russia prepared countermeasures in case of seizure of its assets abroad, according to Medvedev at the St. Petersburg International Legal Forum. (Source: @tass_agency/382239)

The Russian Foreign Ministry, through Lavrov, stated that European proposals for stabilization forces in Ukraine would create a 'new Berlin Wall,' rejecting temporary solutions to the conflict. (Source: @tass_agency/382249, @tass_agency/382236)

A Russian strike on a beach in Zaporizhzhia injured four people including three children, and a strike on Novopetrivka killed a Norwegian People's Aid demining worker and injured four others. (Source: @UkraineNow/82150, @UkraineNow/82149)

Russian forces intensified offensive activity across multiple eastern axes including Lyman, Kupyansk, South Slobozhansk, and North Slobozhansk directions, according to Ukrainian OS spokesman Viktor Trehubov. (Source: @UkraineNow/82138)

On the Sumy direction, Russian assault troops of Army Group 'North' continued small-arms battles in Bachevsk in the Shostka district, while fighting persisted in Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, and Novaya Sech in the Sumy district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127)

Four drones were detected heading toward Sharivka/Kov'yagi in Kharkiv Oblast from the north, and Russian Geranium drones struck military supply railway trains in the Kharkov region. (Source: @eRadarrua/97948, @ukr_leaks_eng/31135)

Two Lancet-type drones targeted Khotyn and Mykolaivka in Sumy Oblast, while KAB bombs were reported on the Khotyn-2 district and drones on Stetskivka/Rudnyvka. (Source: @eRadarrua/97975, @eRadarrua/97978)

A reactive drone was tracked from eastern Dnipropetrovsk Oblast toward northern Zaporizhzhia Oblast, and another drone passed Komyshuvakha heading toward Zaporizhzhia/Vilnyansk. (Source: @kpszsu/66211)
A high-speed target was detected heading east in Zaporizhzhia Oblast and eastern Dnipropetrovska, indicating continued aerial activity in the eastern sector. (Source: @kpszsu/66177)
The State Border Service of Ukraine confirmed there is no current ground invasion threat from Belarus, but stated Minsk is assisting Russia in launching drones. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31138)

Russian forces established control over Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region, with Army Group 'North' assault troops engaged in small-arms combat in Bachevsk in the Shostka district. (Source: @tass_agency/382236, @ukr_leaks_eng/31127)
Drones were detected in the direction of Putyvl in Sumy Oblast, and two Lancet-type drones were reported over Khotyn and Mykolaivka. (Source: @kpszsu/66211, @eRadarrua/97975)
There is a threat of Iskander-M or S-300 ballistic missile launches from Kursk Oblast into Ukrainian territory, according to air raid alerts. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68029)
Drones were detected from the Black Sea coastline toward and past Ochakiv in Mykolaiv Oblast heading in a northern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/66228)
Drones were reported on the northwestern outskirts of Kherson heading in an eastern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/66201)
A sudden blackout occurred across all of Russian-occupied Kherson region, with the occupation governor confirming partial or full de-energization of all districts. (Source: @UkraineNow/82143)
A Russian strike on a beach in Zaporizhzhia injured four people including three children, according to the regional OVA. (Source: @UkraineNow/82150)

A Russian strike on Novopetrivka in Vysokopillya community killed a 24-year-old Norwegian People's Aid demining specialist and injured four other workers, two in critical condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82149)
Russia began transferring air defense systems from other regions to the Kerch Bridge area due to Ukrainian strike threats. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)

A Russian MiG-31K, the carrier platform for the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic ballistic missile, took off from Savastleyka airfield, posing a significant threat as the Kinzhal is not intercepted in most regions. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026)
A reactive Shahed drone was detected heading toward Kremenchuk from the east, and a ballistic missile threat was declared from Kursk Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68030, @mon1tor_ua/68029)

Ukraine destroyed over 60,000 tons of ammunition at a Russian Baltic Fleet arsenal near Saint Petersburg, a significant strike on Russian military logistics infrastructure. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)

The Ukrainian Armed Forces began fielding the domestic 250 kg 'Vyravnyvatel' guided bomb with ranges up to 140 km, though Russian air defenses limit achievable launch altitudes. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125)
Wreckage of two large drones, suspected to be a Ukrainian kamikaze drone, was found on the Black Sea coast of Turkey according to local gendarmerie. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31128)

The US Department of Defense announced it will create two training grounds simulating Ukrainian conflict conditions for electronic warfare training and joint drone development with countermeasure developers. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31121)

key_developments: Russian forces established control over Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region, with ongoing small-arms combat in Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, and Novaya Sech involving Army Group 'North'. (Source: @tass_agency/382236, @ukr_leaks_eng/31127)

Ukraine destroyed over 60,000 tons of Russian ammunition at a Baltic Fleet arsenal near Saint Petersburg, prompting Russian air defense redeployments toward Moscow and Kerch Bridge. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)
A Russian MiG-31K capable of launching Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles took off from Savastleyka airfield, posing a severe threat to Ukrainian targets. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026)
Russian Geranium drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies for Ukrainian forces in the Kharkov region, disrupting logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135)
Russian forces intensified offensive operations across Lyman, Kupyansk, South Slobozhansk, and North Slobozhansk directions according to Ukrainian military officials. (Source: @UkraineNow/82138)

A Russian beach strike in Zaporizhzhia injured four people including three children, and a Novopetrivka strike killed a Norwegian People's Aid demining worker. (Source: @UkraineNow/82150, @UkraineNow/82149)
Russia confirmed domestic fuel supply challenges with refineries at maximum capacity, requiring previously unused reserve mechanisms per Deputy PM Novak's report to Putin. (Source: @UkraineNow/82141)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is intensifying significantly across multiple axes. Russian forces are pressing offensive operations in Sumy (Ivolzhanskoye captured), Kharkiv, and the eastern Zaporizhzhia-Dnipropetrovsk corridor while maintaining pressure on Kherson and Mykolaiv. The confirmed launch of a MiG-31K with Kinzhal capability from Savastleyka represents a high-end strike threat that most Ukrainian air defense regions cannot reliably intercept. Russia's redeployment of air defense assets toward Moscow and Kerch Bridge following the Baltic Fleet arsenal strike suggests Ukrainian deep-strike operations are having a tangible strategic effect on Russian force allocation.
sections.regional_response: The European Union is moving toward deeper defense integration, with European Commissioner for Defence Andrius Kubilius calling for Ukraine's inclusion in a future EU defense union and the merging of European and Ukrainian defense industries. The US Department of Defense is establishing dedicated training grounds simulating Ukrainian combat conditions for electronic warfare and drone development. Meanwhile, Poland is tightening refugee policy by denying temporary protection to Ukrainians from non-combat regions, and Polish PM Tusk publicly characterized Zelensky's absence from the Gdansk reconstruction conference as de-escalation, signaling potential diplomatic friction.
sections.intelligence_notes: The confirmed fielding of the Ukrainian 'Vyravnyvatel' 250 kg guided bomb represents a meaningful capability advancement, though Russian air defense and aviation constraints limit its employment envelope. The discovery of drone wreckage on Turkey's Black Sea coast, suspected to be a Ukrainian kamikaze drone, raises questions about flight path deviations or mission failures near NATO territory. Russia's domestic fuel crisis, with Novak reporting refineries at maximum capacity and requiring unprecedented reserve mechanisms, suggests sanctions and war costs are materially impacting Russian internal infrastructure. The reported Russian overnight strikes on Sumy, Kharkov, and Pavlovgrad district indicate continued deep-strike campaign tempo against rear-area targets.
